raw.behavior: calls re-anchor → extract-slice on every seed record regardless of dispatcher entity_strategy
raw.outcome: extract-slice on interaction-only base produces synthetic 'entity-<recordId>' slugs that fail reverse-lookup verification → escalation.md emitted, run.mts exits code 2 (escalated-re-anchor)

corrected.behavior: classify-text-field-entities (Opus 4.6) classifies Payments.Payer/Payee Name as company role=from-like/to-like; emit-edges substitutes canonical slugs in 4 Payment edges' from/to; extract-text-field-entities emits 5 unique Company entity pages with extraction_strategy=text-field provenance
corrected.outcome: reviewer (Opus 4.6) verdict score=1.0/1.0 across routing/schema/fidelity/classification/dedup/idempotency; user approved

reason: Stage 0 dispatcher correctly returns entity_strategy=none for interaction-only bases — but run.mts fires re-anchor BEFORE consulting the dispatcher's verdict, so legacy extract-slice runs unconditionally on every seed record. For interaction tables (record = event/edge, not noun-entity) the legacy path is structurally incompatible: it generates 'entity-<recordId>' fallback slugs that fail reverse-lookup. The fix: gate re-anchor's extract-slice invocations on dispatcher.entity_strategy ∈ {single-pass, record-by-record, chunked-by-AP} per cluster. Interaction-only clusters (entity_strategy=none) should bypass extract-slice entirely and run S5a → emit-edges → S5b directly. Captured as #237 follow-up; this triple is the canonical example for future #237 work.
